$meta.step: Step 2 — Design System and UX Foundation
$meta.description: Typography primitives. System-first strategy: no font binary is committed. Tabular figures are mandatory for every financial and tabular numeric value.

font.family.sans.value: system-ui, -apple-system, 'Segoe UI', Roboto, 'Noto Sans', 'Helvetica Neue', Arial, sans-serif
font.family.sans.description: System-first sans stack. No font binary is committed to this repository; every surface uses the platform's own UI face. This keeps the download small on low-end Android devices and avoids shipping a licensed binary in a PUBLIC repository.

font.family.mono.value: ui-monospace, 'Roboto Mono', 'DejaVu Sans Mono', 'Courier New', monospace
font.family.mono.description: Monospace stack for receipt previews and any place a fixed column grid carries meaning.

font.feature.tabularNumbers.description: OpenType feature enabling tabular (fixed-width) figures. Mandatory wherever integer Rupiah amounts, weights, quantities or timestamps are stacked in a column, so digits align and a misread total is harder to produce.
font.feature.tabularNumbers.intendedUsage: Money fields, data tables, receipt previews, KPI cards, courier cash reconciliation.

font.size.caption.description: Font size for the caption role. Scales with the platform text-size setting; layouts must survive 200% scaling without losing a primary action.
font.size.caption.accessibilityNotes: Never used as a fixed pixel value that ignores the user's text-size preference.

font.lineHeight.caption.description: Line height paired with font.size.caption. Ratio 1.33.

font.size.label.description: Font size for the label role. Scales with the platform text-size setting; layouts must survive 200% scaling without losing a primary action.
font.size.label.accessibilityNotes: Never used as a fixed pixel value that ignores the user's text-size preference.

font.lineHeight.label.description: Line height paired with font.size.label. Ratio 1.43.

font.weight.bold.description: Bold weight. Weight alone never conveys status.

font.maxLineLength.body.description: Maximum comfortable measure for body copy, in characters. Longer measures reduce reading accuracy on wide Console Web layouts.
